values.user_data:
#!/bin/bash
set -euxo pipefail

# Values rendered by Terraform templatefile()

GLOBAL_PROJECT="mkhan-test"
GLOBAL_ENV="sandbox"

HOSTNAME="apse2-mkhan-test-sandbox-bastion-01"

sudo hostname "$HOSTNAME"
sudo hostnamectl set-hostname "$HOSTNAME"

sudo sed -i "s/^preserve_hostname: false/preserve_hostname: true/" /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg || true

if ! grep -q "$HOSTNAME" /etc/hosts; then
  sudo sed -i "/^127.0.0.1/ s/$/ $HOSTNAME/" /etc/hosts
fi

PKG_MGR=$(command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "dnf" || echo "yum")
sudo $PKG_MGR -y update
sudo $PKG_MGR -y install awscli postgresql15 nc telnet

sudo cat <<'BANNER' > /etc/motd
********************************************************************
*                                                                  *
* WARNING: AUTHORIZED ACCESS ONLY                                  *
*                                                                  *
* This system is the property of the organisation.                 *
* Access is restricted to authorised users only.                   *
*                                                                  *
* All activities on this system are logged and monitored.          *
* Unauthorized access may result in disciplinary action.           *
*                                                                  *
********************************************************************
BANNER

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh
if [[ $- == *i* ]]; then
  [ -f /etc/motd ] && cat /etc/motd
fi
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh
export HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
export PS1='[\u@\h \W]\$ '
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh

echo "User-data completed successfully for $HOSTNAME" | sudo tee /var/log/user-data-complete.log

#!/bin/bash
set -euxo pipefail

# Values rendered by Terraform templatefile()

GLOBAL_PROJECT="mkhan-test"
GLOBAL_ENV="sandbox"

HOSTNAME="apse2-mkhan-test-sandbox-bastion-01"

sudo hostname "$HOSTNAME"
sudo hostnamectl set-hostname "$HOSTNAME"

sudo sed -i "s/^preserve_hostname: false/preserve_hostname: true/" /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg || true

if ! grep -q "$HOSTNAME" /etc/hosts; then
  sudo sed -i "/^127.0.0.1/ s/$/ $HOSTNAME/" /etc/hosts
fi

PKG_MGR=$(command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "dnf" || echo "yum")
sudo $PKG_MGR -y update
sudo $PKG_MGR -y install awscli postgresql15 nc telnet

sudo cat <<'BANNER' > /etc/motd
********************************************************************
*                                                                  *
* WARNING: AUTHORIZED ACCESS ONLY                                  *
*                                                                  *
* This system is the property of the organisation.                 *
* Access is restricted to authorised users only.                   *
*                                                                  *
* All activities on this system are logged and monitored.          *
* Unauthorized access may result in disciplinary action.           *
*                                                                  *
********************************************************************
BANNER

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh
if [[ $- == *i* ]]; then
  [ -f /etc/motd ] && cat /etc/motd
fi
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh
export HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
export PS1='[\u@\h \W]\$ '
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh

echo "User-data completed successfully for $HOSTNAME" | sudo tee /var/log/user-data-complete.log

alb_listener_default_action_type.description: Default action type for the listener

alb_name.description: Name of the Application Load Balancer

alb_security_groups.description: Security group IDs for the ALB

alb_subnet_tier.description: Which tier key to use from alb_subnets_by_tier

alb_target_group_target_type.description: Target type for the target group

tags.description: Tags to apply to resources

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the ALB and target group will be created

ecs_exec_log_group_name.description: CloudWatch Logs group name for ECS Exec session logs

tags.description: Tags to apply to all ECS resources

target_group_arn.description: Target group ARN to attach the ECS service to

vpc_id.description: VPC ID

security_group_owner_id.description: The owner ID of the security group

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the security group will be created

ecs_task_environment.description: Environment variables as a map; converted to ECS list-of-objects

ecs_task_execution_role_arn.description: IAM role ARN used by ECS for task execution

ecs_task_log_group_name.description: CloudWatch log group name for awslogs

ecs_task_operating_system_family.description: ECS Task operating system family (e.g., LINUX, WINDOWS)

ecs_task_secrets.description: Secrets to inject as environment variables (Secrets Manager or SSM).

task_role_arn.description: IAM role ARN used by ECS for task execution

untagged_image_count.description: Number of untagged images to keep before expiring

gateway_id.description: The Internet Gateway ID (for Public RTs)

nat_gateway_id.description: The NAT Gateway ID (for Private RTs)

rt_name.description: The Name tag for the route table

tier.description: The tier this route table belongs to (pub, pvt, data, vpce)

rt_name.description: The Name tag for the route table

tier.description: The tier this route table belongs to (pub, pvt, data, vpce)

vpc_id.description: The VPC ID where the route table will be created

route_table_id.description: The ID of the created route table

gateway_id.description: The Internet Gateway ID (for Public RTs)

nat_gateway_id.description: The NAT Gateway ID (for Private RTs)

rt_name.description: The Name tag for the route table

tier.description: The tier this route table belongs to (pub, pvt, data, vpce)

vpc_id.description: The VPC ID where the route table will be created

prefix_list_id.description: Prefix list ID (useful for SG rules).

name.description: Name tag for the gateway endpoint.

policy_json.description: Optional endpoint policy JSON. Use null for AWS default.

route_table_ids.description: Route tables to associate with this gateway endpoint.

service_name.description: Gateway endpoint service name (S3 or DynamoDB).

tags.description: Common tags.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID.

ami_id.description: AMI ID for the bastion instance.

associate_public_ip_address.description: Should the instance get a public IP? For bastion-in-private, keep false.

security_group_ids.description: Security group for the bastion instance.

ssh_key_name.description: Optional EC2 key pair name. Leave null for SSM-only.

subnet_id.description: Subnet ID for the bastion (typically a shared pvt subnet).

tags.description: Extra tags to apply.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where bastion will be deployed.

sg_description.constant_value: Security Group for SSM Bastion Host

security_group_arn.description: The ARN of the created security group

security_group_id.description: The ID of the created security group

security_group_name.description: The name of the created security group

security_group_owner_id.description: The owner ID of the security group

tags.description: Tags to apply to the security group

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the security group will be created

security_group_id.description: Security group ID created for the DB.

additional_security_group_ids.description: Additional SGs to attach to the DB instance (optional).

allowed_cidr_blocks.description: CIDR blocks allowed to connect to Postgres port (optional).

allowed_security_group_ids.description: Source security groups allowed to connect (optional).

availability_zone.description: Optional fixed AZ (leave empty for AWS-managed). Not recommended with multi_az.

backup_window.description: Preferred backup window (UTC) e.g. 02:00-03:00. Empty = AWS default.

db_name.description: Initial database name to create. Leave empty if you don't want one created.

db_subnet_group_name.description: Name of the DB subnet group to use for the RDS instance.

engine_version.description: Optional explicit Postgres engine version. If empty, module uses latest default.

final_snapshot_identifier.description: Final snapshot identifier if skip_final_snapshot is false.

global_env.description: Environment name (e.g., sandbox, dev, stg, prd).

identifier.description: RDS instance identifier. If empty, defaults to name.

ignore_password_changes.description: If true, ignore password drift in state (useful during interim manual rotation).

kms_key_id.description: Optional KMS Key ARN/ID for storage encryption (leave empty until available).

maintenance_window.description: Preferred maintenance window (UTC) e.g. Mon:04:00-Mon:05:00. Empty = AWS default.

max_allocated_storage.description: Autoscaling storage upper bound in GB (0 disables).

name.description: Base name used for tagging and default identifiers.

password.description: Master password (local for now until KMS/Secrets Manager is available).

publicly_accessible.description: Whether the DB has a public endpoint.

sg_name.description: Optional explicit security group name. If empty, module generates one.

snapshot_identifier.description: If set, restore DB from this snapshot ID; if null, create a fresh instance.

storage_encrypted.description: Encrypt storage. If kms_key_id is empty, AWS-managed key is used when true.

tags.description: Additional tags to merge with defaults.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the RDS security group will be created.

image_tag_mutability.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository (e.g., 'MUTABLE' or 'IMMUTABLE')

untagged_image_count.description: Number of untagged images to keep before expiring

private_zone_id.description: Private hosted zone id (null if not created)

private_zone_name.description: Private hosted zone name (null if not created)

public_zone_id.description: Public hosted zone id (null if not created)

public_zone_name.description: Public hosted zone name (null if not created)

record_fqdns.description: FQDNs of records created by this module

private_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for private hosted zone

public_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for public hosted zone

records.description: DNS records to create. Use either `records` (non-alias) OR `alias`.

tags.description: Tags to apply to Route53 resources where supported

vpc_associations.description: VPCs to associate to the private hosted zone

private_zone_id.description: Private hosted zone id (null if not created)

private_zone_name.description: Private hosted zone name (null if not created)

public_zone_id.description: Public hosted zone id (null if not created)

public_zone_name.description: Public hosted zone name (null if not created)

record_fqdns.description: FQDNs of records created by this module

private_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for private hosted zone

public_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for public hosted zone

records.description: DNS records to create. Use either `records` (non-alias) OR `alias`.

tags.description: Tags to apply to Route53 resources where supported

vpc_associations.description: VPCs to associate to the private hosted zone

mfa_delete.description: Configured MFA delete setting (may require root MFA CLI/API to actually enforce).

mfa_delete_cli_example.description: Example CLI command to enable MFA Delete (root MFA required).

abort_multipart_after_days.description: Abort incomplete multipart uploads after N days.

bucket_name.description: Name of the S3 bucket to create.

deep_archive_days.description: Days after creation to transition to DEEP_ARCHIVE.

enable_default_encryption.description: Enable default server-side encryption for the bucket.

enable_intelligent_tiering.description: Optionally transition objects into INTELLIGENT_TIERING early in lifecycle.

enable_lifecycle.description: Enable S3 lifecycle configuration on this bucket.

enable_noncurrent_version_rules.description: Apply lifecycle rules to noncurrent (versioned) objects.

enable_object_size_limits.description: Enforce object size min/max for PutObject via bucket policy.

force_destroy.description: Allow Terraform to delete a non-empty bucket.

intelligent_tiering_days.description: Days after creation to transition to INTELLIGENT_TIERING (commonly 0 or 30).

kms_key_alias.description: Existing KMS key alias e.g. alias/aws/s3 or alias/mkhan-test-s3 (used when encryption_type is SSE-KMS).

kms_key_arn.description: Optional KMS key ARN for SSE-KMS. If null, AWS managed key is used.

max_object_size_bytes.description: Maximum allowed object size in bytes (0 disables max check).

mfa_delete.description: MFA delete setting for bucket versioning. Default Disabled; enable via CLI with root MFA.

min_object_size_bytes.description: Minimum allowed object size in bytes.

noncurrent_expire_days.description: Days after becoming noncurrent to expire old versions.

noncurrent_to_deep_archive_days.description: Days after becoming noncurrent to transition old versions to DEEP_ARCHIVE.

retention_days.description: Retention period in days (e.g., 2555 for ~7 years).

server_access_log_bucket_id.description: Target bucket ID (name) where server access logs will be delivered (must be same account/region).

server_access_log_prefix.description: Prefix under which to store access logs in the target bucket.

tags.description: Tags to apply to the bucket.

versioning_status.description: S3 versioning status for the bucket. Default is Suspended.

enable_noncurrent_version_rules.description: Apply lifecycle rules to noncurrent (versioned) objects.

enable_object_size_limits.description: Enforce object size min/max for PutObject via bucket policy.

force_destroy.description: Allow Terraform to delete a non-empty bucket.

intelligent_tiering_days.description: Days after creation to transition to INTELLIGENT_TIERING (commonly 0 or 30).

kms_key_alias.description: Existing KMS key alias e.g. alias/aws/s3 or alias/mkhan-test-s3 (used when encryption_type is SSE-KMS).

before.user_data:
#!/bin/bash
set -euxo pipefail

# Values rendered by Terraform templatefile()

GLOBAL_PROJECT="mkhan-test"
GLOBAL_ENV="sandbox"

HOSTNAME="apse2-mkhan-test-sandbox-bastion-01"

sudo hostname "$HOSTNAME"
sudo hostnamectl set-hostname "$HOSTNAME"

sudo sed -i "s/^preserve_hostname: false/preserve_hostname: true/" /etc/cloud/cloud.cfg || true

if ! grep -q "$HOSTNAME" /etc/hosts; then
  sudo sed -i "/^127.0.0.1/ s/$/ $HOSTNAME/" /etc/hosts
fi

PKG_MGR=$(command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "dnf" || echo "yum")
sudo $PKG_MGR -y update
sudo $PKG_MGR -y install awscli postgresql15 nc telnet

sudo cat <<'BANNER' > /etc/motd
********************************************************************
*                                                                  *
* WARNING: AUTHORIZED ACCESS ONLY                                  *
*                                                                  *
* This system is the property of the organisation.                 *
* Access is restricted to authorised users only.                   *
*                                                                  *
* All activities on this system are logged and monitored.          *
* Unauthorized access may result in disciplinary action.           *
*                                                                  *
********************************************************************
BANNER

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh
if [[ $- == *i* ]]; then
  [ -f /etc/motd ] && cat /etc/motd
fi
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/ssm-banner.sh

sudo cat <<'EOF' > /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh
export HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
export PS1='[\u@\h \W]\$ '
EOF
sudo chmod 0644 /etc/profile.d/00-bastion-prompt.sh

echo "User-data completed successfully for $HOSTNAME" | sudo tee /var/log/user-data-complete.log

ingress_rules.description: HTTPs allowed inbound to VPCE PL

precondition.error_message: Provide at least 2 subnets for tier '${var.alb_subnet_tier}' in alb_subnets_by_tier (for multi-AZ ALB).

data.name: ${var.db_sn_grp_name}
data.description: Subnet group for RDS instances in the data tier

source_sgs.for_each: ${{for idx , sg_id in var.allowed_security_group_ids : idx => sg_id}}

precondition.error_message: Record ${each.key} references zone_type=${each.value.zone_type} but that zone is not created.
Record ${each.key} must specify exactly one of: `records` (non-alias) OR `alias`.
Record ${each.key} references zone_type=${each.value.zone_type} but that zone is not created.
Record ${each.key} must specify exactly one of: `records` (non-alias) OR `alias`.

tier_settings.az1: ${{for idx , tier in local.tiers : tier => merge(var.az1_tier_settings[tier], {'base': '${local.tier_base_blocks[idx]}'})}}
tier_settings.az2: ${{for idx , tier in local.tiers : tier => merge(var.az2_tier_settings[tier], {'base': '${local.tier_base_blocks[idx + length(local.tiers)]}'})}}

/project/locals.tf.subnet_maps: ${{for az_name , settings in local.tier_settings : az_name => merge([for tier , s in settings : {for i in range(s.count) : "${tier}-${i + 1}" => {'cidr': '${cidrsubnet(s.base, 1, i)}', 'tier': '${tier}', 'suffix': '${format("%02d", i + 1)}', 'is_public': '${s.is_pub && tier == "pub"}'}}])}}

subnet_ids.az1: ${{for k , m in module.az1_subnets : k => m.subnet_id}}
subnet_ids.az2: ${{for k , m in module.az2_subnets : k => m.subnet_id}}

canonical.az1: ${{for tier , key in local.subnet_key : tier => try(local.subnet_ids.az1[key], None)}}
canonical.az2: ${{for tier , key in local.subnet_key : tier => try(local.subnet_ids.az2[key], None)}}

by_tier.az1: ${{for tier in local.tiers : tier => [for k in sort([for kk in keys(local.subnet_ids.az1) : kk if can(regex("^${tier}-", kk))]) : local.subnet_ids.az1[k]]}}
by_tier.az2: ${{for tier in local.tiers : tier => [for k in sort([for kk in keys(local.subnet_ids.az2) : kk if can(regex("^${tier}-", kk))]) : local.subnet_ids.az2[k]]}}

/project/modules/network/sg/locals.tf.ingress_cidr_flat: ${flatten([for idx , rule in var.ingress_rules : [for cidr in try(rule.cidr_blocks, []) : {'key': '${idx}-cidr-${cidr}', 'from_port': '${coalesce(try(rule.from_port, None), rule.to_port)}', 'to_port': '${rule.to_port}', 'protocol': '${rule.protocol}', 'cidr_ipv4': '${cidr}'}]])}
/project/modules/network/sg/locals.tf.ingress_sg_flat: ${[for idx , rule in var.ingress_rules : {'key': '${idx}-sg', 'from_port': '${coalesce(try(rule.from_port, None), rule.to_port)}', 'to_port': '${rule.to_port}', 'protocol': '${rule.protocol}', 'src_sg_id': '${rule.source_security_group_id}'} if try(rule.source_security_group_id, None) != None]}
/project/modules/network/sg/locals.tf.egress_rules_flat: ${flatten([for idx , rule in var.egress_rules : [for cidr in rule.cidr_blocks : {'key': '${idx}-${cidr}', 'from_port': '${coalesce(try(rule.from_port, None), rule.to_port)}', 'to_port': '${rule.to_port}', 'protocol': '${rule.protocol}', 'cidr_ipv4': '${cidr}'}]])}

/project/modules/compute/ecs/ecstask/loc.port_mappings_list: ${[for pm in var.ecs_task_port_mappings : {'containerPort': '${pm.container_port}', 'hostPort': '${coalesce(try(pm.host_port, None), pm.container_port)}', 'protocol': '${lower(coalesce(try(pm.protocol, None), "tcp"))}'}]}

/project/modules/compute/alb/locals.tf.alb_subnets_by_tier_ids: ${{for tier , v in var.alb_subnets_by_tier : tier => try(v.ids, v)}}

aws_profile.description: Provide SSO login at run-time through tfvars

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the security group will be created

tags.description: Tags to apply to the security group

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where bastion will be deployed.

subnet_id.description: Subnet ID for the bastion (typically a shared pvt subnet).

ami_id.description: AMI ID for the bastion instance.

ssh_key_name.description: Optional EC2 key pair name. Leave null for SSM-only.

associate_public_ip_address.description: Should the instance get a public IP? For bastion-in-private, keep false.

tags.description: Extra tags to apply.

security_group_ids.description: Security group for the bastion instance.

untagged_image_count.description: Number of untagged images to keep before expiring

image_tag_mutability.description: The tag mutability setting for the repository (e.g., 'MUTABLE' or 'IMMUTABLE')

ecs_task_operating_system_family.description: ECS Task operating system family (e.g., LINUX, WINDOWS)

ecs_task_cpu_architecture.description: ECS Task CPU architecture (e.g., X86_64, ARM64)

ecs_task_container_name.description: Container name (defaults to ecs_task_family_name when null)

ecs_task_environment.description: Environment variables as a map; converted to ECS list-of-objects

ecs_task_log_group_name.description: CloudWatch log group name for awslogs

ecs_task_secrets.description: Secrets to inject as environment variables (Secrets Manager or SSM).

task_role_arn.description: IAM role ARN used by ECS for task execution

ecs_task_execution_role_arn.description: IAM role ARN used by ECS for task execution

untagged_image_count.description: Number of untagged images to keep before expiring

ecs_exec_log_group_name.description: CloudWatch Logs group name for ECS Exec session logs

tags.description: Tags to apply to all ECS resources

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the ALB and target group will be created

alb_name.description: Name of the Application Load Balancer

alb_security_groups.description: Security group IDs for the ALB

alb_subnets_by_tier.description: Map of tier => aws_subnets data object (has .ids) OR list(string) subnet IDs

alb_subnet_tier.description: Which tier key to use from alb_subnets_by_tier

validation.error_message: alb_subnet_tier must exist as a key in alb_subnets_by_tier.

alb_listener_default_action_type.description: Default action type for the listener

alb_target_group_target_type.description: Target type for the target group

tags.description: Tags to apply to resources

vpc_id.description: VPC ID

target_group_arn.description: Target group ARN to attach the ECS service to

validation.error_message: deployment_controller_type must be ECS or CODE_DEPLOY.

vpc_id.description: The ID of the VPC where this subnet will be created

enable_resource_name_dns_a_record_on_lau.description: Whether to enable DNS A record creation on launch for instances in this subnet. Only applies to public subnets.

route_table_id.description: The ID of the route table to associate with the subnet

subnet_id.description: The Subnet ID to associate with this route table

vpc_id.description: The VPC ID where the route table will be created

rt_name.description: The Name tag for the route table

tier.description: The tier this route table belongs to (pub, pvt, data, vpce)

gateway_id.description: The Internet Gateway ID (for Public RTs)

nat_gateway_id.description: The NAT Gateway ID (for Private RTs)

subnet_ids.description: List of subnet IDs to include in the DB subnet group.

db_sn_grp_name.description: Name for the RDS DB Subnet Group.

tags.description: Extra tags to apply.

name.description: Base name used for tagging and default identifiers.

global_env.description: Environment name (e.g., sandbox, dev, stg, prd).

tags.description: Additional tags to merge with defaults.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID where the RDS security group will be created.

sg_name.description: Optional explicit security group name. If empty, module generates one.

allowed_cidr_blocks.description: CIDR blocks allowed to connect to Postgres port (optional).

allowed_security_group_ids.description: Source security groups allowed to connect (optional).

additional_security_group_ids.description: Additional SGs to attach to the DB instance (optional).

identifier.description: RDS instance identifier. If empty, defaults to name.

db_name.description: Initial database name to create. Leave empty if you don't want one created.

password.description: Master password (local for now until KMS/Secrets Manager is available).

engine_version.description: Optional explicit Postgres engine version. If empty, module uses latest default.

max_allocated_storage.description: Autoscaling storage upper bound in GB (0 disables).

storage_encrypted.description: Encrypt storage. If kms_key_id is empty, AWS-managed key is used when true.

kms_key_id.description: Optional KMS Key ARN/ID for storage encryption (leave empty until available).

publicly_accessible.description: Whether the DB has a public endpoint.

availability_zone.description: Optional fixed AZ (leave empty for AWS-managed). Not recommended with multi_az.

backup_window.description: Preferred backup window (UTC) e.g. 02:00-03:00. Empty = AWS default.

maintenance_window.description: Preferred maintenance window (UTC) e.g. Mon:04:00-Mon:05:00. Empty = AWS default.

final_snapshot_identifier.description: Final snapshot identifier if skip_final_snapshot is false.

ignore_password_changes.description: If true, ignore password drift in state (useful during interim manual rotation).

db_subnet_group_name.description: Name of the DB subnet group to use for the RDS instance.

snapshot_identifier.description: If set, restore DB from this snapshot ID; if null, create a fresh instance.

tags.description: Tags to apply to Route53 resources where supported

public_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for public hosted zone

private_zone_comment.description: Optional comment for private hosted zone

vpc_associations.description: VPCs to associate to the private hosted zone

records.description: DNS records to create. Use either `records` (non-alias) OR `alias`.

bucket_name.description: Name of the S3 bucket to create.

tags.description: Tags to apply to the bucket.

force_destroy.description: Allow Terraform to delete a non-empty bucket.

versioning_status.description: S3 versioning status for the bucket. Default is Suspended.

mfa_delete.description: MFA delete setting for bucket versioning. Default Disabled; enable via CLI with root MFA.

server_access_log_bucket_id.description: Target bucket ID (name) where server access logs will be delivered (must be same account/region).

server_access_log_prefix.description: Prefix under which to store access logs in the target bucket.

enable_lifecycle.description: Enable S3 lifecycle configuration on this bucket.

retention_days.description: Retention period in days (e.g., 2555 for ~7 years).

enable_intelligent_tiering.description: Optionally transition objects into INTELLIGENT_TIERING early in lifecycle.

intelligent_tiering_days.description: Days after creation to transition to INTELLIGENT_TIERING (commonly 0 or 30).

deep_archive_days.description: Days after creation to transition to DEEP_ARCHIVE.

abort_multipart_after_days.description: Abort incomplete multipart uploads after N days.

enable_noncurrent_version_rules.description: Apply lifecycle rules to noncurrent (versioned) objects.

noncurrent_to_deep_archive_days.description: Days after becoming noncurrent to transition old versions to DEEP_ARCHIVE.

noncurrent_expire_days.description: Days after becoming noncurrent to expire old versions.

enable_object_size_limits.description: Enforce object size min/max for PutObject via bucket policy.

min_object_size_bytes.description: Minimum allowed object size in bytes.

max_object_size_bytes.description: Maximum allowed object size in bytes (0 disables max check).

enable_default_encryption.description: Enable default server-side encryption for the bucket.

kms_key_arn.description: Optional KMS key ARN for SSE-KMS. If null, AWS managed key is used.

kms_key_alias.description: Existing KMS key alias e.g. alias/aws/s3 or alias/mkhan-test-s3 (used when encryption_type is SSE-KMS).

name.description: Name tag for the gateway endpoint.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID.

service_name.description: Gateway endpoint service name (S3 or DynamoDB).

route_table_ids.description: Route tables to associate with this gateway endpoint.

policy_json.description: Optional endpoint policy JSON. Use null for AWS default.

tags.description: Common tags.

name.description: Name tag for the interface endpoint.

vpc_id.description: VPC ID.

security_group_ids.description: Security groups attached to the endpoint ENIs.

private_dns_enabled.description: Enable Private DNS for AWS services where supported.

policy_json.description: Optional endpoint policy JSON. Use null for AWS default.

tags.description: Common tags.
